basic_details.job_title: TGPSC Assistant Scientist Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 20 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Telangana Public Service Commission (TGPSC) Assistant Scientist (Analyst Grade-II) Notification 2026 has been released on 20 April 2026 on the official website tgpsc.gov.in. A total of 20 vacancies have been announced through the TGPSC Assistant Scientist Recruitment 2026.

important_dates.admit_card_date: From 7 days prior to the Examination and upto 5:00 PM of the previous day of the Examination

qualification.details: Educational Qualification (Essential): Must possess basically a Bachelor’s Degree with Chemistry as one of the subjects from any recognized University in India. | Age Limit: | Minimum Age: 18 years Maximum Age: 34 years (as on 01/07/2026) | Age Relaxation: | SC / ST / BCs & EWS : 5 Years Physically Handicapped persons : 10 Years Telangana State Government Employees : Up to 5 Years based on the length of regular service Ex-Servicemen : 3 Years & length of service rendered in the armed forces N.C.C. (who have worked as Instructor in N.C.C.) : 3 Years & length of service rendered in the N.C.C.

selection_process: Written Examination (Objective Type) No. of Questions Duration (Minutes) Maximum Marks
PAPER-I: General Studies And General Abilities 150 150 150

The selection of candidates for appointment to the posts will be made by Written Examination (Objective Type) by CBRT / OMR based and the Selection for the posts will be based on marks secured in the written examination.
Sportspersons & EWS - not less than 40% BCs - not less than 35% SCs
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must apply online only. The step-by-step process is as follows:
Step 1 - Visit Official Website: Log on to https://www.tgpsc.gov.in.
Step 2 - One Time Registration (OTR): If not registered earlier, complete OTR to obtain TGPSC ID.
Step 3 - Fill Online Application: Login with TGPSC ID, fill all required details accurately.
Step 4 - Pay Application Fee: Pay the prescribed fee online through the payment gateway.
Step 5 - Submit & Print: Preview, confirm & submit the application. Download and take a printout of the PDF application form for future reference.
Note: Submission of ONLINE Applications is mandatory. Applicants are advised to submit online applications well before the closing date and not wait till the last date.
important_instructions: Submission of ONLINE Applications is mandatory. Applicants are advised to submit online applications well before the closing date and not wait till the last date. One Time Registration (OTR) is not considered as an application for any post under any circumstances. The applicants need to submit ONLINE Applications for this Notification specifically. The applicants are advised to retain their mobile number and email-ID registered in OTR for receiving OTP/SMS for further communication till the finalization of the recruitment process of this notification. The Commission reserves the right to conduct the Written Examination (Objective type) either in OMR based Offline mode or in Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT) mode. The Commission will normalize the scores of candidates for the Computer Based Tests which are conducted in Multi-sessions. The applicant is required to visit the Commission’s website regularly to keep himself/herself updated until completion of the recruitment process. Any application sent through any mode other than the prescribed online mode shall not be entertained under any circumstances. The Written Examination (Objective Type) is tentatively scheduled to be held during July - 2026. The Commission will not make any changes / improvements in the application form submitted by the applicant. Handwritten / Typed / Photostat copies / outside printed Application Form will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in TGPSC Assistant Scientist (Analyst Grade‑II) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Telangana Public Service Commission has released 20 vacancies for the post of Assistant Scientist (Analyst Grade‑II) in the Telangana Pollution Control Board (TPCB) under Notification No. 01/OG/PC/2026 dated 20.04.2026.

faq.question: Q2. What are the online application dates for TGPSC Assistant Scientist Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window opens on 27 April 2026 and the last date and time for submission of online applications is 25 May 2026 up to 5:00 PM on the official website tgpsc.gov.in.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Assistant Scientist (Analyst Grade‑II)?
faq.answer: Applicants must possess a Bachelor’s Degree with Chemistry as one of the subjects from any recognised University in India as on the date of notification (20.04.2026), and they must satisfy all other conditions of eligibility prescribed by TGPSC.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit and age relaxation for TGPSC Assistant Scientist 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 34 years as on 01 July 2026. Relaxations are: SC/ST/BCs & EWS: 5 years Persons with Disabilities: 10 years Telangana State Government Employees: up to 5 years based on length of regular service Ex‑Servicemen: 3 years plus length of service in armed forces NCC instructors: 3 years plus length of NCC service.

faq.question: Q5. What is the pay scale for TGPSC Assistant Scientist (Analyst Grade‑II)?
faq.answer: The post carries a scale of pay of Rs. 45,960 – 1,24,150 as per the Telangana state pay structure for this cadre.

faq.question: Q6. How can I apply online for TGPSC Assistant Scientist (Analyst Grade‑II) Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must first complete One Time Registration (OTR) on tgpsc.gov.in (if not already registered), then login with their TGPSC ID, fill the Assistant Scientist online application, upload photograph and signature, pay the required fee through the online payment gateway, and finally submit and download the application PDF for future reference; OTR alone is not an application for this post.

faq.question: Q7. What are the minimum qualifying marks for TGPSC Assistant Scientist written exam?
faq.answer: The minimum qualifying marks are 40% for OC, Sportspersons and EWS, 35% for BCs and 30% for SCs, STs and PHs, and only candidates securing these minimum percentages (after normalization in case of multi‑session CBRT) will be considered for selection.

faq.question: Q8. When will the TGPSC Assistant Scientist 2026 hall tickets and exam be conducted?
faq.answer: Hall tickets can be downloaded from 7 days prior to the written examination and up to 5:00 PM of the previous day of the exam from the Commission’s website, and the Written Examination (Objective Type) is tentatively scheduled during July 2026.
